# AeroSandbox root oneshot

Wrap AeroSandbox's `Atmosphere` class in one shot and call a real instance method.

## Why This Case Exists

Many engineering libraries are object-oriented, so a realistic wrapper system has to create, use, and close sessions without hand-written package adapters.

## Case Shape

- ID: `aerosandbox_root`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `package`
- Workflow Slice: `mission`
- Target Kind: `package`
- Upstream Target: `aerosandbox`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/aerosandbox_root/challenge.toml`

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=aerosandbox_root
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only aerosandbox_root
```

## Fixtures

This case does not need checked-in fixtures.

## Smoke Flow

### 1. Create an Atmosphere session

Tool: `create_atmosphere`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "altitude": 1000.0
}
```

Captures: `{'atmosphere_session_id': 'session_id'}`

### 2. Compute density from the live session

Tool: `atmosphere_density`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "session_id": "{atmosphere_session_id}"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['1.'].

### 3. Close the Atmosphere session

Tool: `close_atmosphere`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "session_id": "{atmosphere_session_id}"
}
```

## What This Case Proves

- One-shot package ingestion can discover a useful class from a broad upstream namespace.
- Session-style wrapping works for a real aerospace object lifecycle.
- The generated facade can execute a meaningful physics method deterministically.

## Known Limits

- This case exercises a lightweight object interaction, not a full multi-component workflow.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: pull a small physics object out of a larger aerospace package.
# This case shows class/session wrapping on a real upstream engineering library.
id = "aerosandbox_root"
title = "AeroSandbox root oneshot"
tier = "local_full"
style = "package"
slice = "mission"

[example]
summary = "Wrap AeroSandbox's `Atmosphere` class in one shot and call a real instance method."
motivation = "Many engineering libraries are object-oriented, so a realistic wrapper system has to create, use, and close sessions without hand-written package adapters."
proves = [
  "One-shot package ingestion can discover a useful class from a broad upstream namespace.",
  "Session-style wrapping works for a real aerospace object lifecycle.",
  "The generated facade can execute a meaningful physics method deterministically.",
]
limitations = [
  "This case exercises a lightweight object interaction, not a full multi-component workflow.",
]

[target]
kind = "package"
value = "aerosandbox"

[probe]
imports = ["aerosandbox"]

[scaffold]
kind = "package"
symbol_include_patterns = ["^Atmosphere$"]
max_generated_tools = 16

[smoke]
[[smoke.steps]]
label = "Create an Atmosphere session"
tool = "create_atmosphere"
arguments = { altitude = 1000.0 }
capture_json = { atmosphere_session_id = "session_id" }

[[smoke.steps]]
label = "Compute density from the live session"
tool = "atmosphere_density"
arguments = { session_id = "{atmosphere_session_id}" }

[smoke.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["1."]

[[smoke.steps]]
label = "Close the Atmosphere session"
tool = "close_atmosphere"
arguments = { session_id = "{atmosphere_session_id}" }
```
